rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Leave-k-out jackknife structural stability for cliques

Source: R/cliques.R
clique_stability.Rd

Tests how structurally robust each clique is to species removal. The analysis removes k = 1, 2, ..., max_k species at a time, re-runs full clique detection on each reduced species set, and checks whether matching gene assignments are preserved (Jaccard similarity). ALL cliques are tested, regardless of trait composition. Trait annotations are added post-hoc if species_trait is provided.

clique_stability(
  edges,
  target_species,
  species_trait = NULL,
  all_species = target_species,
  full_cliques = NULL,
  min_species = length(target_species),
  max_k = length(all_species) - 2L,
  max_genes_per_sp = 10L,
  jaccard_threshold = 0.8,
  edge_type = "conserved",
  n_cores = 1L
)

Arguments

edges

Data frame (same format as find_cliques).

target_species

Character vector of species that define clique membership.

species_trait

Optional named character or factor vector mapping species to trait groups. Names must include all all_species. If provided, trait annotations (traits, sole_rep) are added to the output. If NULL (default), trait columns are NA.

all_species

Character vector of ALL species in the analysis universe (default: target_species). Leave-k-out subsets are drawn from all_species. Must be a superset of target_species. When larger than target_species, removing a non-target species tests whether the clique signal is robust to changes in the broader phylogenetic context.

full_cliques

Output of find_cliques, or NULL to compute internally (default).

min_species

Minimum species per clique in the full dataset (default: length(target_species)). During leave-k-out, reduced cliques require only 2 species (the minimum meaningful clique size); a clique is testable if at least 2 of its species remain active.

max_k

Maximum number of species to leave out (default: length(all_species) - 2, leaving at least 2 species).

max_genes_per_sp

Maximum genes per species per HOG (default 10).

jaccard_threshold

Minimum Jaccard similarity for matching reduced-dataset cliques to full-dataset cliques (default 0.8).

edge_type

Edge type filter (default "conserved").

n_cores

Number of OpenMP threads (default 1).

Value

A list with components:

stability

Data frame with columns: clique_idx (1-based), hog, k, n_subsets, n_stable, stability_score, species_present, traits, sole_rep. One row per (clique, k) pair.

clique_disruption

Data frame with columns: species, n_cliques_disrupted (k=1 only), and optionally trait_value if species_trait is provided. One row per species in all_species.

stability_class

Integer vector (length = number of cliques): highest k at which each clique is structurally stable across ALL subsets (0 = unstable at k=1)

novel_cliques

Integer: total count of novel cliques across all subsets

Details

How it works

For each combination of k species removed from all_species (k = 1, ..., max_k):

  1. All edges involving the removed species are dropped

  2. Clique detection re-runs among remaining target species

  3. Reduced cliques are matched to full-dataset cliques by Jaccard similarity of gene assignments (considering only non-removed species)

A clique is testable in a subset if at least 2 of its species remain active. It is stable at level k if its gene assignments are preserved across ALL C(N, k) subsets where it is testable.

Stability is purely structural — trait annotations are orthogonal and added post-hoc from species_trait if provided.

sole_rep column

The sole_rep column is TRUE if this clique has a single trait value and that trait value has only one target species representative. Only populated when species_trait is provided.

full_cliques parameter

When full_cliques = NULL (default), cliques are computed internally via find_cliques. You can also precompute them:


fc <- find_cliques(edges, target_species)
stab <- clique_stability(edges, target_species,
                         full_cliques = fc)

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Structural stability for all cliques
cliques <- find_cliques(edges, all_sp, min_species = 2L)
stab    <- clique_stability(edges, all_sp,
                            full_cliques = cliques)

# With trait annotation
trait <- setNames(rep(c("annual", "perennial"), each = 4), all_sp)
stab  <- clique_stability(edges, all_sp,
                          species_trait = trait,
                          full_cliques = cliques)

# Cliques surviving any single species dropout
k1 <- stab$stability[stab$stability$k == 1, ]
stable_cliques <- cliques[k1$clique_idx[k1$stability_score == 1], ]

# Multi-level: stability_class >= 2 survives any pair of dropouts
deeply_stable <- which(stab$stability_class >= 2)
} # }
